purpose: Query CloudTrail or Azure Activity Log for historical events — who did what, when, and from where.
description: Searches CloudTrail event history or Azure Activity Log. Use for: who stopped my EC2, who created or deleted a resource, user activity history, API call records, detect unauthorized actions. Multi-step required for multiple event types (CloudTrail constraint: one event-name per step).
capabilities: query CloudTrail for specific API events with full filter support (event-name, user, IP, resource, error-code)
query Azure Activity Log for resource operation history
detect who performed a specific action (stopped, terminated, created, deleted a resource)
surface failed or unauthorized API calls via error-code filtering
return compact or full event records with requester identity and source IP
context_descriptions: event filter — CloudTrail event-name (e.g. StopInstances) or Azure resource-provider
actor filter — username or IAM principal who performed the action
time range — audit window with exact ISO-8601 start and end timestamps
resource filter — specific resource name or type to narrow audit results
